name: Brittany Michelle Pedersen
bio: Brittany Michelle Pedersen is running uncontested for a 13th Subcircuit seat. She faces a striking background: three DUI charges (one dismissed, two reduced to reckless driving). She previously failed judicial bids in Kane County while living there — receiving negative bar ratings — and subsequently moved to Cook County to run again. She has stated publicly: "I believe that people deserve second chances, and third chances, and maybe sometimes a fourth." She will win this seat unopposed.
background: Attorney. Moved from Kane County to Cook County to run for judge. Previously ran in Kane County with negative bar ratings.

bar_ratings.alliance_detail: Ratings not available from Alliance — previously received negative ratings in Kane County judicial bids

red_flags.label: 3 DUI charges — 1 dismissed, 2 reduced to reckless driving
red_flags.detail: Brittany Michelle Pedersen has faced three DUI charges during her legal career. One charge was dismissed and two were reduced to reckless driving. An attorney seeking to become a judge is held to a higher standard of conduct; these charges raise questions about her judgment and fitness for the bench.

red_flags.label: Moved to Cook County specifically to run; previously failed judicial bids in Kane County with negative ratings
red_flags.detail: Pedersen previously sought judgeships in Kane County — where she lived — and received negative bar ratings. She then relocated to Cook County and is now running uncontested for a Cook County circuit court seat. Her own public quote: "I believe that people deserve second chances, and third chances, and maybe sometimes a fourth." The legal community's prior negative assessments raise concerns about qualifications that venue-shopping cannot solve.
